name: Rockwell Automation Customer Experience Centre
description: 面向制造业客户的工业自动化与数字化转型体验中心，展示AI赋能的工厂与生产系统。
why_matters: 这是典型传统工业硬件与自动化方案，不属于形态创新AI硬件，但体现了AI在制造业落地的实际需求。
latest_news: 2024: 在新加坡揭幕 Customer Experience Centre，推动本地创新向全球扩展。

name: FJ Dynamics
description: 面向农业、建筑与测绘场景的机器人与智能装备公司，主打物理世界自动化。
why_matters: 属于传统机器人硬件，不是新型AI载体形态，但代表亚洲物理AI产业链的核心方向。
latest_news: 2026: 出现在东亚公司排名列表中，覆盖农业、建筑和空间信息等业务。

name: Flick
description: Flick 是一个面向电影制作人的 AI filmmaking 平台，帮助创作者用 AI 更高效地完成内容制作流程。
description_en: Flick is an AI filmmaking platform for filmmakers, helping creators produce content more efficiently with AI.
why_matters: 该公司已进入 Y Combinator，并完成 600 万美元种子轮融资；它瞄准的是 filmmaking 这一高频创作场景，用 AI 直接提升影片制作效率，而不是只做单点工具。
why_matters_en: The company joined Y Combinator and raised a $6M seed round; it targets the filmmaking workflow directly, using AI to improve production efficiency rather than offering a narrow point solution.
latest_news: 2026-05: Business Insider 报道其完成 600 万美元种子轮融资，并披露其 AI filmmaking 产品与 pitch deck。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Business Insider reported its $6M seed round and highlighted its AI filmmaking product and pitch deck.

name: alugha
description: alugha 是一家曼海姆的多语言视频平台公司，提供 AI 赋能的视频本地化与多语内容分发能力。
description_en: alugha is a Mannheim-based multilingual video platform company that provides AI-enabled video localization and multilingual content distribution.
why_matters: 尽管这不是传统 VC 融资，但约 100 万欧元研发资助说明其 AI 视频本地化能力获得了公共资金支持；在欧洲市场，这类“替代美国方案”的定位有明确差异化。
why_matters_en: While not a traditional VC round, the roughly €1M R&D grant shows public backing for its AI video localization capability; its positioning as a European alternative to US providers gives it a clear niche.
latest_news: 2026-05: 曼海姆的 alugha GmbH 获得约 100 万欧元 Forschungszulage，用于其多语言视频平台与 AI 视频技术。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Mannheim-based alugha GmbH received roughly €1M in R&D grant funding for its multilingual video platform and AI video technology.

name: Smart Robotics AI Picking Systems
description: 面向仓储分拣的 AI 机器人抓取系统，用于提升电商和物流场景的自动化拣选效率。
why_matters: 聚焦单一高价值仓储场景，商业落地路径清晰，是 AI+机器人效率提升的典型方向。
latest_news: 2026-05: 完成 €10M Series A 融资。

name: Synthetic
description: Synthetic 正在打造一款全自动 AI bookkeeper，目标是无需人工直接介入即可生成 accrual-based financials，面向会计自动化场景。
description_en: Synthetic is building a fully autonomous AI bookkeeper designed to generate accrual-based financials without direct human involvement.
why_matters: TechCrunch 报道其已获得 1000 万美元 seed 轮融资，且产品瞄准“完全自治”的财务记账，这是会计自动化里难度很高、差异化很强的方向。
why_matters_en: TechCrunch reports a $10M seed round, and the product targets a fully autonomous bookkeeping workflow, which is a highly differentiated and technically demanding finance automation category.
latest_news: 2026-05: TechCrunch 报道 Synthetic 获得 1000 万美元 seed 融资，正在设计一款可自动生成 accrual-based financials 的 AI bookkeeper。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: TechCrunch reported that Synthetic raised a $10M seed round to design an AI bookkeeper that can generate accrual-based financials.

name: Origin Lab
description: Origin Lab 正在把游戏公司产生的数据变成可供 world model builders 使用的供给侧数据源，连接 video game 与 AI 训练数据市场。
description_en: Origin Lab is turning video game industry data into a supply source for world model builders, linking games to AI training data markets.
why_matters: 8 百万美元 seed 轮由 Lightspeed 领投，方向是把视频游戏数据卖给 world model builders，这是一种相对少见的数据供给型创新路径。
why_matters_en: The startup raised an $8M seed round led by Lightspeed, and its thesis—selling video game data to world model builders—is an uncommon data-supply innovation.
latest_news: 2026-05: TechCrunch 报道 Origin Lab 完成 800 万美元 seed 融资，帮助游戏公司向 world model builders 出售数据。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: TechCrunch reported Origin Lab raised an $8M seed round to help video game companies sell data to world model builders.

name: Atech
description: Atech 正在把 vibe coding 概念带入 hardware 开发流程，尝试让硬件创建过程像软件生成一样更快、更自然。
description_en: Atech is bringing vibe coding to hardware development, aiming to make hardware creation faster and more intuitive like software generation.
why_matters: 虽然融资规模不大，但它把“vibe coding”从软件扩展到 hardware 制造流程，是一个很清晰的新概念迁移，值得早期跟踪。
why_matters_en: While the funding is small, it extends the vibe-coding concept from software into hardware creation, which is a clear early-stage innovation worth tracking.
latest_news: 2026-05: TechCrunch 报道 Lovable 参与了 Atech 的 80 万美元 pre-seed 轮，这家公司想把 vibe coding 引入硬件开发。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: TechCrunch reported Lovable backed Atech in an $800K pre-seed round to bring vibe coding to hardware.

name: Deep-Tech Startups Support Program (DTSU)
description: DTSU 是 NEDO 面向深科技创业公司的分阶段 R&D 与商业化支持计划，覆盖长周期高风险技术验证与落地。
description_en: DTSU is NEDO's phased R&D and commercialization support program for deep-tech startups, covering long-cycle, high-risk technology validation and deployment.
why_matters: 虽然它不是创业公司产品，但对日本深科技 AI 团队非常关键：单个项目最高可达25亿日元支持，并强调三阶段 R&D 到商业化衔接，适合早期 AI 基础模型、机器人和产业 AI 团队申请。
why_matters_en: Although not a startup product, it is highly relevant for Japanese deep-tech AI teams: projects can receive up to ¥2.5 billion in support with a three-phase path from R&D to commercialization, making it valuable for early foundation-model, robotics, and industrial AI teams.
latest_news: 2026-05: NEDO 继续通过 DTSU 为深科技创业公司提供分阶段研发与商业化支持。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: NEDO continued to support deep-tech startups through DTSU with phased R&D and commercialization funding.

name: Scope
description: Scope 是一家伦敦创业公司，正在打造用于工业巡检的 AI workflow platform，面向复杂工业场景提升检查效率与自动化水平。
description_en: Scope is a London startup building an AI workflow platform for industrial inspections, aiming to improve efficiency and automation in complex industrial settings.
why_matters: Scope 由 Index Ventures 领投拿到 2000 万美元融资，结合其工业巡检 AI workflow 方向，属于典型的高客单价 B2B 深度场景。
why_matters_en: Scope raised $20M led by Index Ventures, and its AI workflow platform for industrial inspections targets a high-value B2B deep workflow niche.
latest_news: 2026-05: 获 Index Ventures 领投 2000 万美元融资。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Secured a $20M round led by Index Ventures.

top_comments_sample: I think this is a cool project. I see a lot of use cases for this, for cases where it is preferable to keep issues local to the repo, distributed via git only, and not the least for all kinds of personal task management. Avoiding the context switching to a web based tool is a nice plus.

There was a small surge in popularity in distributed git issue trackers a bit over a decade ago, and all of them had some sort of problem baked in to the design that made them not very good. Two weeks ago I had listed out the problems I could remember offhand: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47956979 It sounds like there's intentionally no attempt to handle the last one (that this is by devs for devs), and points 3 and 4 might be addressed somehow since it mentions syncing automatically. Does it store data separate from git to avoid the first two?

It’s very slick, but I would be interested to know how separable the UI and the data layer are. I love vim but asking a collaborative group to all use a TUI is difficult. A local web server would be a nice alternative UI
I really like the idea of a distributed issue tracker. I'd encourage you to look at git-bug if you haven't already done so for some prior art / inspiration / hard learned lessons.

> Agent interactions > The MCP server lets AI tools interact with Epiq in a predictable way. Or maybe just publish a skill for the agent to use your CLI? The agent alredy uses CLI commands to interact with git itself
I would prefer a single binary and skill over mcp.

You have my upvote because I love Git-based apps. There’s something cool about Git being an effective database with loads of free hosting options. I’d (re)consider a couple of things if you intend to work on it and make it viable for a wider audience. 1. Who is it aimed for? If product managers and designers _are_ in scope e.g. you imagine full engineering teams using it, then a TUI isn’t gonna cut it. It’s a great interface choice for devs but I don’t think it’s organizationally viable to force everyone else in the terminal. 2. I’d think about either having a central issues repository as a default / recommended option or creating an easy way for linking issues together across repos. To me,
Really elegant solution to the distributed issue state problem. Using user-scoped immutable event logs to prevent git conflicts is a clever architectural choice. Congrats on the launch!

> Conflict handling model: Later events take precedence when conflicts occur Do I understand correctly that if 2 people add a lot of information to one issue only one of them 'wins' and becomes visible? Or is it more subtle? If only the latter one becomes visible, how do you get to the edits of the other person and 'merge' it again?
cool Project but global npm install is a no for me, and there seem to be no similar to python venvs in node.js ecosystem.

community_verdict.summary: 这些评论总体上认可这个项目的思路，认为基于 Git 的分布式 issue tracker 很有吸引力，也有实际用途。很多人同时在讨论它的可用性和产品形态，比如是否要支持网页界面、数据层如何与 UI 解耦，以及是否适合团队协作。也有评论提出了实现层面的疑问和改进建议，包括冲突处理、同步机制、与 git-bug 等已有方案对比，以及安装方式是否足够友好。 用户反馈既包含认可，也提出了执行层面的质疑。 对 PM 来说，关键是验证需求强度与可持续增长。

name: NIKKO Dynamic Electronic Badge
description: 一款可显示图片和动画、支持 AI 及近距离分享的电子徽章硬件。
why_matters: 徽章作为 AI 载体的思路较少见，适合做轻社交、身份展示和内容分享场景。

name: Startup SG Tech
description: Startup SG Tech 是新加坡面向创新科技创业公司的政府资助项目，帮助具商业化潜力的 deep tech 团队获得最高 SGD 500,000 支持。
description_en: Startup SG Tech is a Singapore government funding program for innovative technology startups with commercial potential, offering up to SGD 500,000 in support.
why_matters: 这是一个明确的创业融资入口，单个项目最高可拿到 SGD 500,000，适合深科技团队做研发、原型验证和商业化推进，但它本身是资助项目而非 AI 产品公司。
why_matters_en: This is a concrete startup funding gateway offering up to SGD 500,000 per project, useful for deep-tech teams validating R&D and commercialization, but it is a grant program rather than an AI product company.
latest_news: 2026-05: FundFluent 页面更新了 Startup SG Tech 资助信息，强调该计划面向具商业潜力的创新技术解决方案。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: FundFluent updated its Startup SG Tech grant page, highlighting support for innovative technology solutions with commercial potential.

name: Wirestock
description: Wirestock 正在为 AI labs 提供可用于训练的创意多模态数据供应服务，帮助模型获取更丰富的图像、视频等内容。
description_en: Wirestock is building a creative multimodal data supply business for AI labs, helping models access richer image, video, and other content.
why_matters: 公司刚完成 2300 万美元 Series A，由 Nava Ventures 领投，切入的是 AI 训练数据供给这一关键但高门槛赛道；如果能持续扩张数据来源与授权网络，会直接影响多模态模型训练质量。
why_matters_en: The company just raised a $23M Series A led by Nava Ventures, targeting the critical and hard-to-build AI training data supply layer; scaling its rights-cleared content network could directly improve multimodal model training quality.
latest_news: 2026-05: 完成 2300 万美元 Series A，继续扩展面向 AI labs 的创意多模态数据业务。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Closed a $23M Series A and is expanding its creative multimodal data business for AI labs.

name: AntAI
description: 一家日本 AI 服务公司，与 GUILD 合作推出可按企业课题定制的 AI Hackathon 方案，强调共创与落地转化。
description_en: A Japanese AI services company that co-launched a customized AI hackathon program with GUILD for enterprise co-creation and implementation.
why_matters: 它不是通用咨询，而是把企业课题、招募、场地与活动运营打包成标准化但可定制的 AI 共创服务；不过公开信息未见融资、增长或大规模市场验证，因此评分维持在 Rising Star。
why_matters_en: It packages enterprise problem discovery, recruitment, venue coordination, and event operations into a standardized yet customizable AI co-creation service, but there is no public funding or scale signal, so it remains a Rising Star.
latest_news: 2026-05: 开始提供“パーソナライズドAIハッカソン”企业定制服务。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Started offering the enterprise-customized “Personalized AI Hackathon” service.

top_comments_sample: I wonder how this would be looked upon by the ever changing rules of claude code. If someone from Anthropic sees this, would love to know if I can use my max plan here.

Hi, this looks really powerful, in that it seems to have many use cases. One question I had: Does every sandbox change end (when ready for production) in a pull request? If marketing sends me a pull request and I hate the code, what's the flow like for me to fix it?

I have a suggestion - an assistant which can help to set up all these agents, perhaps based on templates. You already covered various use cases, but it's not clear if it's something concrete. I think a lot of people who might be interested in this product might be interested in an easy set-up process. Even if it doesn't really save time for an experienced ops person, a lot of people would rather talk to a bot than fill a form.

Really cool! We're working on something similar over at https://norisessions.com/ A few questions - you mention proxying keys. One issue that we run into is that there are a bunch of tools that are really useful but require keys to be on disk (e.g. aws cli -- yes yes you can do IAM permissions but still). How do you guys think about those? (Especially since your setup onboarding is 'just install from npm or mise') - poking around on the github, saw that you guys were at one point on fly.io. Did you guys end up switching off them? What motivated that if so? - the CLI integration is cool! Is that actually teleporting remote sessions down to a local machine? Or is it more a window into the remo
Checked license it said copyrighted which makes this unsuable for me. my question is what advantage does this have over real FOSS agentic sandboxes
This is amazing, what was the inspiration?
Congrats on the launch! Tiny bit of feedback on the demo video: the beginning makes me dizzy and I'm not sure what's going on (was watching on my phone).

all of our customers are using Anthropic APIs for programmatic use. Codex and other providers let you use Oauth. But inside of a sandbox, you can technically use max plan since it is the same as using Claude locally.

Thanks! Lots of use cases. For the workflow you mentioned, the idea is marketing sends you a PR with the live preview. You can see the UI changes and if you need to change anything, you can open the session, which will let you modify, backtrack, or continue their work inside the same sandbox they used.

The sandbox’s lifecycle is independent of the PR so that the same user or someone else in your team, like an engineer can come back to the same session and keep building until the result is ready to merge. So you can choose to iterate on the first drafts on the same sandbox. Curious, what flow you envision in your case?

name: Imperagen
description: Imperagen 是一家将 quantum physics 与 AI 结合来重塑 enzyme engineering 的生物技术公司，目标是加速酶设计与优化流程。
description_en: Imperagen is a biotech company combining quantum physics and AI to reshape enzyme engineering and speed up enzyme design and optimization.
why_matters: 公司刚完成 £5 million seed，由 PXN Ventures 领投，累计融资达 £8.5 million；它把 quantum physics 和 AI 用于 enzyme engineering，属于较明确的类别创新，适合追踪后续在生物制造和药物研发上的落地效果。
why_matters_en: The company just raised a £5 million seed round led by PXN Ventures, bringing total funding to £8.5 million. It applies quantum physics and AI to enzyme engineering, making it a notable category innovator with potential impact in biomanufacturing and drug discovery.
latest_news: 2026-05: 完成 £5 million seed 融资，资金将用于继续招人并推进 AI specialists 扩充与产品研发。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Closed a £5 million seed round to hire more AI specialists and continue product development.

name: IrisGo
description: IrisGo 正在打造一款 PC 端的 AI desktop buddy，能够学习用户日常工作流，并在少量甚至无需人工提示的情况下自动执行任务。
description_en: IrisGo is building an AI desktop buddy for PCs that learns daily workflows and automates tasks with limited to no human prompting.
why_matters: 这家公司已获得由 Andrew Ng 的 AI Fund 领投的 $2.8 million seed，定位是面向 PC 的持续型桌面 Agent，而不是单次问答工具；如果能稳定捕捉工作流，它可能切入个人生产力自动化的新入口。
why_matters_en: The company has already raised a $2.8 million seed round led by Andrew Ng's AI Fund. It is positioning itself as a persistent desktop agent for PCs rather than a one-off chatbot, which could make it a new entry point for personal productivity automation if it reliably captures workflows.
latest_news: 2026-05: 公开披露由 Andrew Ng 支持的 $2.8 million seed 融资，并描述其桌面自动化产品方向。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Publicly disclosed a $2.8 million seed round backed by Andrew Ng and outlined its desktop automation product direction.

why_matters: 以眼镜作为 AI 入口，主打免手持拍摄与自然语言问答，推动智能穿戴从通知设备走向日常助手。

name: Doozy Robotics
description: Doozy Robotics 是一家新加坡的 physical AI 人形机器人公司，专注于构建可自主执行工业任务的自动化劳动力。
description_en: Doozy Robotics is a Singapore-based physical AI humanoid company building autonomous industrial workforces.
why_matters: 该公司刚完成种子轮融资，并已宣布面向美国、GCC 和亚洲的全球扩张，说明其产品已从原型走向商业化落地阶段，适合继续跟踪其工业机器人执行力与客户验证进展。
why_matters_en: The company has just raised seed funding and announced global expansion across the US, GCC, and Asia, suggesting it is moving from prototype toward commercial deployment and is worth tracking for industrial robotics execution and customer validation.
latest_news: 2026-05: 宣布获得种子融资，并启动面向美国、GCC 和亚洲的全球扩张，计划在 Series A 前加速增长。
latest_news_en: 2026-05: Announced seed funding and launched a global expansion across the US, GCC, and Asia ahead of a planned Series A.
